tasks.title: Setup Project Repository and Initial Structure
tasks.description: Initialize the project repository with backend and frontend directories, set up basic README, .gitignore, and environment configuration files.
tasks.details: Create a root directory with backend (FastAPI, SQLModel, SQLite) and frontend (React, Vite, shadcn-ui, TypeScript) subdirectories. Add README.md, .gitignore, and environment variable templates. Use Poetry for Python dependency management and npm/yarn for Node.js. Recommended: Python 3.11+, Node.js 18+, Poetry 1.7+, npm 9+.

subtasks.title: Initialize Backend with Poetry and Dependencies
subtasks.description: Set up the backend environment using Poetry, including initializing the project and adding required dependencies.
subtasks.details: Navigate to the backend directory, run 'poetry init' to create the project, and add necessary dependencies using 'poetry add'. Ensure the backend is ready for development.

subtasks.title: Initialize Frontend with npm/yarn and Dependencies
subtasks.description: Set up the frontend environment by initializing with npm or yarn and installing required dependencies.
subtasks.details: Navigate to the frontend directory, run 'npm init' or 'yarn init', and install essential packages. This prepares the frontend for further development.

subtasks.title: Add README, .gitignore, and Environment Templates

tasks.title: Backend Core Infrastructure Setup
tasks.description: Configure FastAPI, SQLModel, and SQLite for the backend, including database connection and initial models, using a file-based SQLite database for maximum simplicity.
tasks.details: Set up FastAPI (latest stable), SQLModel (latest), and SQLite. Define database connection using SQLModel with SQLite file in the project directory (e.g., ./data/app.db). Create initial models (User, Employee, Absence, AbsenceType, Holiday) as per PRD schema. Use environment variables for DATABASE_URL (e.g., engine = create_engine(os.getenv('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///./data/app.db'))). No complex migrations—only simple table creation. File-based DB for easy Docker integration. No separate DB containers or services. Development without Docker (only at the end). Production mode only—no environment-specific configurations. Implement DB backup/reset functions in the settings menu[1][2][4].
tasks.testStrategy: Test database connection and model creation with pytest. Verify models can be created and queried. Test backup and reset functionality.

subtasks.title: Install and Configure FastAPI
subtasks.description: Set up a Python virtual environment, install FastAPI and Uvicorn, and create a basic FastAPI application file.
subtasks.details: Create a project directory, initialize a virtual environment, install FastAPI and Uvicorn using pip, and create a main.py file with a minimal FastAPI app.

subtasks.title: Set Up SQLModel and Database Connection
subtasks.description: Install SQLModel and configure the database connection settings in the project.
subtasks.details: Install SQLModel and a compatible database driver (e.g., for SQLite). Set up the database URL and connection logic in the project files. Use SQLite file in the project directory (e.g., ./data/app.db).

subtasks.title: Define Initial Models
subtasks.description: Create initial SQLModel models representing the database schema.
subtasks.details: Define Python classes using SQLModel to represent tables and relationships for the application's data.

subtasks.title: Configure Environment Variables
subtasks.description: Set up environment variables for configuration, such as database URLs and secret keys.
subtasks.details: Create a .env file or use another method to securely store and load environment-specific settings, ensuring sensitive data is not hardcoded. Use DATABASE_URL pointing to SQLite file (e.g., sqlite:///./data/app.db).

subtasks.title: Initialize Database and Migrations
subtasks.description: Initialize the database and set up migration tools to manage schema changes.
subtasks.details: No complex migrations—only simple table creation. Use SQLModel.metadata.create_all(engine) to initialize the database schema.

subtasks.title: Implement DB Backup and Reset Functions
subtasks.description: Add backup and reset functionality for the SQLite database in the settings menu.
subtasks.details: Implement functions to backup and reset the SQLite database file, accessible via the application settings menu.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test backup and reset functionality.

tasks.title: Authentication System Implementation
tasks.description: Implement a simple single-user authentication system with hardcoded credentials, bcrypt password verification, and JWT-based session management. No registration, roles, or multi-user support.
tasks.details: Implement a FastAPI login endpoint that accepts the hardcoded username '<ADMIN_USERNAME>' and password '<ADMIN_PASSWORD>' (bcrypt hash: $2b$12$s6pk...m8). On successful authentication, issue a JWT token for session management. No user registration, roles, or complex user management. All authentication logic should be as simple as possible, focusing on a single user with full access. Use environment variables for JWT secret and bcrypt rounds.

tasks.title: Absence Management System Implementation

tasks.title: Restructure Employee Data Model: Separate Master and Vacation Data
tasks.description: Refactor the current Employee model to split immutable master data and annual vacation data into separate tables, extend employee fields, and update the UI for improved clarity and management.
tasks.details:
1. **Backend Data Model:**
   - Create a new `Employee` table for master data: `id`, `title`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `position`, `email`, `birth_date`, `date_hired`, `federal_state`, `active`.
   - Create a `VacationAllowance` table: `id`, `employee_id` (FK), `year`, `annual_allowance`, `carryover_days`, `total_allowance`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.
   - Define a `FederalState` enum covering all 16 German states.
   - Write migrations to split existing employee data and migrate vacation-related fields to the new structure.
2. **API Layer:**
   - Update schemas, endpoints, and validation to support the new structure.
   - Ensure endpoints allow CRUD for both master and vacation data, and support filtering by year and employee.
3. **Frontend:**
   - Update TypeScript interfaces to match the new backend models.
   - Refactor UI components:
     - Employee list: compact view with name, position, date hired, status.
     - Employee detail: full master data and vacation data management, with clear separation.
     - Integrate federal state selection and display.
   - Ensure responsive design for desktop, tablet, and mobile.
4. **Data Migration:**
   - Implement scripts or migration logic to move existing data to the new structure without loss.
5. **Documentation:**
   - Document the new data model, API changes, and UI updates for future maintainability.
tasks.testStrategy:
- Run database migrations and verify data integrity: all employees have correct master and vacation data split.
- Test all CRUD operations for both Employee and VacationAllowance via API and UI.
- Confirm that the employee list displays only the required fields and is responsive.
- Verify that the detail view allows editing all master and vacation data, and that changes persist.
- Check that federal state selection works and is stored correctly.
- Perform regression tests to ensure no loss of functionality in employee management.
- Validate data migration by comparing pre- and post-migration records for accuracy.

<info added on 2025-07-23T12:37:51.977Z>
The implementation plan for Subtask 17.13 "Performance & Usability Test" is defined as follows:

PHASE 1: AUTOMATED PERFORMANCE TESTS  
- Conduct fully automated performance tests using an AI agent.  
- API Load Testing with Locust (Python-based) simulating concurrent users at levels 1, 5, 10, 25, and 50. Test GET/POST/PUT endpoints for employees, vacation-allowances, and auth. Target response time under 200ms (95th percentile) and error rate below 1%.  
- Database Performance Testing with large datasets (1000+ employees, 5000+ vacation allowance entries) focusing on complex queries, JOINs, pagination, and search/filter performance to ensure stable operation under load.  
- Frontend Performance Testing using Lighthouse CI and Bundle Analyzer targeting Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1, and bundle size under 500KB.  
- Memory and Resource Monitoring on backend (memory leaks, CPU usage, DB connection leaks) and frontend (JavaScript memory, DOM nodes, event listener leaks).

PHASE 2: MANUAL USABILITY TESTS  
- AI agent generates detailed test checklists and evaluation criteria.  
- User (Maria) executes tests and provides structured feedback including status (✅/❌/⚠️), rating (1-5), comments, and screenshots.  
- Manual test areas include:  
  - Core Workflow Testing: creating/editing employees with new fields, managing vacation allowances (CRUD), and testing search/filter functions.  
  - Responsive Design Testing across desktop (1920x1080, 1366x768), tablet (768x1024), and mobile (375x667 iPhone, 360x640 Android) devices, evaluating layout, touch usability, and readability.  
  - Accessibility Testing for keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios, focus indicators, and alt texts.  
  - Error Handling & Edge Cases: invalid inputs, network interruptions, long texts, session timeouts, and concurrent editing conflict resolution.

COORDINATION PROTOCOL  
- Stepwise process: AI runs automated tests and analyzes results, provides initial manual test checklist, user performs tests and submits feedback, AI documents feedback and issues next checklist, repeating until all manual tests complete, followed by final evaluation and documentation.

SUCCESS CRITERIA  
- Performance: API response times under 200ms (95th percentile), frontend LCP under 2.5s, bundle size under 500KB, no memory leaks, stable performance with 25+ concurrent users.  
- Usability: all workflows intuitive, responsive design functional on all tested devices, accessibility compliant with WCAG 2.1 AA, and user-friendly error handling.

This detailed plan is ready for execution in the defined sequence to comprehensively assess performance and usability of the Employee Data Model restructure.

tasks.title: Implement German School Holidays Import by Federal State
tasks.description: Integrate German school holidays into the absence calendar with support for all 16 federal states using the ferien-api.de service to automatically import holiday data.
tasks.details:
1. Backend Integration:
- Implement a service module to fetch school holiday data from ferien-api.de using their REST API endpoints (e.g., https://ferien-api.de/api/v1/holidays/{stateCode}/{year}).
- Map the 16 German federal states to their respective two-letter state codes as required by the API.
- Design logic to import holidays for multiple years, ideally covering the current year and upcoming years as needed.
- Parse and transform the API response JSON into the internal holiday data model, including start and end dates, holiday names, and federal state association.
- Extend the existing holiday database schema if necessary to store school holidays with federal state linkage.
- Implement scheduled or on-demand import functionality to keep school holiday data up to date.

2. API and Data Model:
- Extend the Holiday API to expose school holidays per federal state.
- Ensure the API supports querying holidays by federal state and year.

3. Frontend:
- Update the absence calendar UI to display school holidays per employee's federal state.
- Provide configuration options to enable or disable school holiday visibility.

4. Error Handling and Logging:
- Handle API failures gracefully with retries and error logging.
- Validate data integrity before saving to the database.

5. Documentation:
- Document the new import feature, API endpoints, and configuration options.

Code Example Snippet (Python):
```python
import requests

def fetch_school_holidays(state_code: str, year: int):
    url = f'https://ferien-api.de/api/v1/holidays/{state_code}/{year}'
    response = requests.get(url)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.json()
```

Use this data to update the database accordingly.

tasks.title: Implement Automatic Monthly Sync for German School Holidays via mehr-schulferien.de API
tasks.description: Develop a complete system to automatically synchronize German school holiday data monthly using the mehr-schulferien.de API, including database integration, admin interface, frontend enhancements, and technical services orchestration.
tasks.details:
1. Automatic Sync Mechanism:
- Implement a monthly scheduled job using APScheduler or Celery to fetch school holiday data for all 16 German federal states from mehr-schulferien.de API v2.1.
- Develop a SchoolHolidayApiClient to handle API requests efficiently, respecting rate limits and error handling.
- Create a SchoolHolidayDiffService to detect differences between local database records and API data, enabling intelligent incremental updates.
- Implement bulk update logic to process data for years 2023 through at least 2030 and beyond.
- Design conflict resolution strategies for data discrepancies, including manual override options.
- Add rollback mechanisms to revert database changes if API errors or inconsistencies occur during sync.

2. Database Integration:
- Extend the existing holidays table to include fields: holiday_type ENUM ('PUBLIC_HOLIDAY', 'SCHOOL_VACATION'), federal_state_code, school_vacation_type, data_source, and last_updated timestamp.
- Optimize database performance with appropriate indices on federal_state_code, holiday_type, and last_updated.
- Use batch processing for efficient bulk inserts and updates.

3. Admin Interface:
- Build a dashboard displaying sync status, history logs, and error reports.
- Provide manual sync trigger functionality for immediate data refresh.
- Implement a conflict resolution UI to review and resolve data conflicts.
- Integrate monitoring and alerting mechanisms to notify admins of sync failures or anomalies.

4. Frontend Integration:
- Add federal state selection in employee profiles to personalize holiday views.
- Visually distinguish between public holidays and school vacations in the calendar UI.
- Enable filtering options for school vacations.
- Provide configurable display settings for holiday types.

5. Technical Services:
- Develop modular services: SchoolHolidaySyncService for orchestration, SchoolHolidayApiClient for API communication, SchoolHolidayDiffService for change detection.

tasks.title: Implement Multi-Year Holiday Management with Hybrid Automatic Import and Scheduler

tasks.title: Implementiere Google-Kalender-ähnliches Design mit Sidebar-Filtern für Kalenderansicht
tasks.description: Entwickle eine Kalenderansicht mit Sidebar-Filtern, die Google-Kalender nachempfunden ist, inklusive Mitarbeiter- und Kalenderfiltern mit individuellen Farben und responsivem Design.
tasks.details:
### Backend
- Erweitere die `employees` Tabelle um eine Spalte `calendar_color` (VARCHAR) für individuelle Farben.
- Erweitere die `calendar_settings` Tabelle um Filter-Felder: `show_holidays`, `show_school_vacations`, `show_vacation_absences`, `show_sick_leave`, `show_training`, `show_special_leave` (alle boolean) und `visible_employee_ids` (JSON array).
- Implementiere API-Endpunkte zum Speichern und Laden der Filter-Einstellungen sowie zum Abrufen der Mitarbeiterliste mit Farben.

### Frontend
- Erstelle eine `CalendarSidebar.tsx` Komponente (250px breit, collapsible) mit zwei Hauptbereichen: 'Meine Kalender' und 'Weitere Kalender'.
- Entwickle eine wiederverwendbare `CalendarFilterSection.tsx` Komponente für die Filterbereiche.
- Passe das Layout an, um eine Grid-Struktur mit Sidebar und Kalender zu verwenden.
- Implementiere Filter-State-Management mit React Context oder Zustand.
- Filtere Events basierend auf den Sidebar-Auswahlen und wende Farb-Mapping für Mitarbeiter-Events an.
- Gestalte die Komponenten im Stil von Google Kalender mit collapsible Sections, Checkboxen mit Farbindikatoren, Hover-Effekten und Smooth Transitions.
- Stelle sicher, dass die Sidebar auf Mobile ausblendbar ist.

### Nice-to-have
- Integriere einen Color Picker für Mitarbeiterfarben.
- Füge 'Alle anzeigen/ausblenden' Buttons hinzu.
- Implementiere eine Suchfunktion für Mitarbeiter.
- Ermögliche das Speichern von Filter-Presets.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Überprüfe, ob die Sidebar korrekt angezeigt und ein-/ausgeklappt werden kann.
2. Teste, ob die Filter-Einstellungen korrekt gespeichert und geladen werden.
3. Verifiziere, dass Events basierend auf den Filtern korrekt angezeigt oder ausgeblendet werden.
4. Prüfe, ob die Farben der Mitarbeiter-Events korrekt zugewiesen werden.
5. Teste die Responsivität der Sidebar auf verschiedenen Geräten.
6. Überprüfe die Funktionalität von Nice-to-have-Features wie Color Picker, Suchfunktion und Filter-Presets.
